message:
fix: suppress hydration warnings on root elements

Add suppressHydrationWarning to <html> and <body> elements.

Browser extensions (password managers, ad blockers, accessibility
tools, etc.) commonly inject attributes or elements into these
root tags, causing React hydration mismatch errors (#418).

This is the standard fix recommended by React/Next.js for this
issue since the application code cannot control browser extensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
